agency: New York City Department of Transportation (DOT)
method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, respecting robots.txt — nyc.gov disallows only /html/misc/). Fingerprinted the legacy .shtml main site (Akamai/AWS/Dynatrace) and the nycstreetdesign.info Drupal 9 microsite (Pantheon). Enumerated the full DOT Open Data inventory via the Socrata Discovery API using the VERIFIED agency label 'Department of Transportation (DOT)' (267 assets). Pulled column schemas for the top entity datasets.
headline_findings: DOT is the most open-data-rich domain in the project: 267 verified Open Data assets under agency label 'Department of Transportation (DOT)'.
But it is 178 flat datasets + 64 MAP-ONLY visualizations (zero queryable columns) + 18 external links — scattered snapshots, no owned or unified DOT API.
Nearly every street asset carries a LION SegmentID (bike routes, traffic counts, closures), yet DOT exposes no queryable street-network API keyed on it.
The one transaction — applying for a street-work permit — runs through the NYCSTREET portal (nycstreets.net) with NO public API; only the issued-permit snapshot (tqtj-sjs8) reaches Open Data.
Two disconnected web stacks: legacy .shtml main site (Akamai/AWS/Dynatrace) and a modern Drupal 9 street-design microsite on Pantheon.
Motor Vehicle Collisions datasets are NYPD, not DOT; DOT's safety layer is Vision Zero View (v7f4-yzyg). See apis-observed.md and tech-stack.md.

apis_observed.note: Per-dataset SODA/GeoJSON for all 267 DOT assets; flat snapshots + map-only visualizations; no cross-dataset resource model or SegmentID-joined network.
Used to enumerate DOT inventory by Dataset-Information_Agency.
Street-opening/construction permit applications; NO public API. The net-new write surface.
Street Design Guidelines; JSON:API-capable but serves manual content, not street assets.

fruit.name: Bike Routes / cycling network
opendata_match: New York City Bike Routes mzxg-pwib (25c)
fruit.notes: Lanes, paths, greenways, signed routes; keyed on bikeId + LION SegmentID. Also a map-only twin.

fruit.name: Traffic & pedestrian signals
opendata_match: Traffic Signal and All-Way Stop Study Requests w76s-c5u4 (57c)
fruit.notes: No single 'all traffic signals' dataset — only accessible/APS, Barnes Dance, LPI location sets and a study-request intake. Signal inventory is a gap.

fruit.name: Curbside parking regulations & signs
opendata_match: Parking Regulation Locations and Signs nfid-uabd (25c)
fruit.notes: Every posted curbside sign + regulation text, positioned along a blockface.

fruit.name: Speed humps (Vision Zero)
fruit.notes: Traffic-calming installations by blockface.

fruit.name: Pedestrian plazas
opendata_match: NYC DOT Pedestrian Plazas - Polygon k5k6-6jex (19c)
NYC DOT Pedestrian Plazas - Point Feature 5dck-9m6g (24c)
fruit.notes: Reclaimed street space maintained with community partners; area/perimeter + partner.

fruit.name: Bridges & condition ratings
fruit.notes: DOT-inventoried structures keyed on BIN, with latest numeric + verbal condition rating.

fruit.name: Street-work / street-opening permits
fruit.machine_readable: Open Data snapshot only (read); no write API
opendata_match: Street Closures due to Construction Activities by Block i6b5-j7bu (11c)
fruit.notes: Applications go through the NYCSTREET portal with NO public API; only issued permits are re-published to Open Data. The one net-new write surface for this domain.

fruit.name: Truck routes
opendata_match: New York City Truck Routes (Map) wnu3-egq7
fruit.notes: The single most-viewed DOT asset (86k views) is a MAP-ONLY visualization with zero queryable columns — emblematic of the 64 map assets whose geometry has no query surface. Modeled loosely as a street-network segment resource.

fruit.name: Automated traffic volume counts
fruit.notes: Segment-level volume counts keyed on SegmentID — a strong example of the LION join spine (mapped to the network, not a dedicated entity schema here).

fruit.name: Street Design Guidelines
fruit.notes: The design manual lives in a separate modern Drupal 9 microsite on Pantheon — content, not street assets; disconnected from the data.

fruit.name: Street Network Changes
fruit.notes: DOT street-network change log keyed to the LION spine; from the DOT datafeeds page.

fruit.name: Street Network Changes - Metadata
fruit.notes: Column/field metadata dictionary for the Street Network Changes feed.

fruit.name: NYC DOT Bi-Annual Pedestrian Index
fruit.notes: Twice-yearly pedestrian volume index at benchmark locations citywide.

fruit.name: Transportation Data Collection Calendar
fruit.notes: Schedule of DOT traffic/pedestrian/bike data-collection activities.

fruit.name: Local Law 9 Data (2024-2025)
fruit.notes: Local Law 9 mandated reporting dataset.

fruit.notes: Quarterly bike-share ridership usage metrics reported to DOT.
